workflow_name: Customer Renewal Email -> Forecast Impact + Path
user_message: I have this escalation email from Fourth Coffee that needs triage this. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 08:51 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fourth Coffee account health

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 16:35 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for account health. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 15:59 EST
Subject: Re: Re: account health Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for account health. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fourth Coffee: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

required_artifacts: path forward with MACC/funding options if applicable

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF35-N trap (No native 'renewal motion' workflow in skill set -- current design synthesizes); produces complete Contract state summary with freshness, forecast impact with confidence, response draft (no commercia
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF35-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF35-N failure mode (No native 'renewal motion' workflow in skill set -- current design synthesizes)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF35-N -- No native 'renewal motion' workflow in skill set -- current design synthesizes
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
